name: blackscholes
documentation:
# blackscholes

![](https://img.shields.io/pypi/dm/blackscholes) | 
![](https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/blackscholes) | 
![](https://img.shields.io/codecov/c/github/carlolepelaars/blackscholes)

A Black-Scholes calculator for Python that includes up to the third-order Greeks.

Supports the Black-Scholes-Merton model, 
Black-76 model and option structures.

## Installation

`pip install blackscholes`

## Examples

### Input variables
```python3
S = 55.0  # Asset price of 55
K = 50.0  # Strike price of 50
T = 1.0  # 1 Year to maturity
r = 0.0025  # 0.25% Risk-free rate
sigma = 0.15  # 15% Volatility
q = 0.0 # 0% Annual Dividend Yield
```

### Call

```python3
from blackscholes import BlackScholesCall
call = BlackScholesCall(S=S, K=K, T=T, r=r, sigma=sigma, q=q)
call.price()  ## 6.339408
call.delta()  ## 0.766407
call.spot_delta() ## 0.7683
call.charm()  ## 0.083267
```

### Put

```python3
from blackscholes import BlackScholesPut
put = BlackScholesPut(S=S, K=K, T=T, r=r, sigma=sigma, q=q)
put.price()  ## 1.214564
put.delta()  ## -0.23359
put.spot_delta() ## -0.23417
put.charm()  ## 0.083267
```

### Black-76

The Black-76 model is often used specifically for options and futures and bonds.
`blackscholes` also supports this model. To see all available greeks
check out section [4. The Greeks (Black-76)](https://carlolepelaars.github.io/blackscholes/4.the_greeks_black76).

**Call Example**
```python
from blackscholes import Black76Call
call = Black76Call(F=55, K=50, T=1, r=0.0025, sigma=0.15)
call.price()  ## 6.2345
call.delta()  ## 0.7594
call.vomma()  ## 45.1347
```

**Put Example**
```python
from blackscholes import Black76Put
put = Black76Put(F=55, K=50, T=1, r=0.0025, sigma=0.15)
put.price()  ## 1.2470
put.delta()  ## -0.2381
put.vomma()  ## 45.1347
```

### Structures

`blackscholes` offers the following six option structures:
- Straddle
- Strangle
- Butterfly
- Iron Condor
- Spreads
- Iron Butterfly

All structures have a long and short version. To learn more
check out section [6. Option Structures](https://carlolepelaars.github.io/blackscholes/6.option_structures).

**Long Straddle Example**
```python3
from blackscholes import BlackScholesStraddleLong

straddle = BlackScholesStraddleLong(S=55, K=50, T=1.0,
                                    r=0.0025, sigma=0.15)
straddle.price()  ## 7.5539
straddle.delta()  ## 0.5328
```

### Binary options

Binary options are also called exotic, digital or bet options. `blackscholes` supports Greeks for binary calls and puts.

## Contributing

We very much welcome new contributions! Check out the [Github Issues](https://github.com/CarloLepelaars/blackscholes/issues)
to see what is currently being worked on.

Also check out [Contributing](https://carlolepelaars.github.io/blackscholes/contributing) in the documentation 
to learn more about 
contributing to [blackscholes](https://github.com/CarloLepelaars/blackscholes).

classes.name: blackscholes.strangle.BlackScholesStrangleLong

methods.name: blackscholes.strangle.BlackScholesStrangleLong._calc_attr
documentation:
Combines attributes from a put and call option into a long strangle.

All greeks and price are combined in the same way.

```
param attribute_name

String name of option attribute
```

pointing to a method that can be called on
BlackScholesCall and BlackScholesPut.

```
return

Combined value according to long strangle.
```

Create long strangle option structure.

- Long strangle -> Put(K1) + Call(K2)

```
param S

Price of underlying asset

param K1

Strike price for put

param K2

Strike price for call
```

It must hold that K1 < K2.

```
param T

Time till expiration in years (1/12 indicates 1 month)

param r

Risk-free interest rate (0.05 indicates 5%)

param sigma

Volatility (standard deviation) of stock (0.15 indicates 15%)

param q

Annual dividend yield (0.05 indicates 5% yield)
```

classes.name: blackscholes.spread.BlackScholesCalendarCallSpread

methods.name: blackscholes.spread.BlackScholesCalendarCallSpread._calc_attr
documentation:
Combines attributes from two put options into a calendar spread.

All greeks and prices are combined in the same way.

```
param attribute_name

String name of option attribute
```

pointing to a method that can be called on
BlackScholesCall and BlackScholesPut.

```
return

Combined value according to the calendar spread.
```

Create a calendar call spread option structure.

Horizontal Calendar Call Spread -> K1 == K2

Diagonal Calendar Call Spread -> K1 != K2

```
param S

Price of underlying asset

param K1

Strike price for 1st call to buy.

param K2

Strike price for 2nd call to sell.

param T1

Time till expiration in years for 1st call.

param T2

Time till expiration in years for 2nd call.
```

(1/12 indicates 1 month)

```
param r

Risk-free interest rate (0.05 indicates 5%)

param sigma

Volatility (standard deviation) of stock (0.15 indicates 15%)

param q

Annual dividend yield (0.05 indicates 5% yield)
```

classes.name: blackscholes.put.BlackScholesPut

methods.name: blackscholes.put.BlackScholesPut.price
documentation: Fair value of a Black-Scholes put option.

methods.name: blackscholes.put.BlackScholesPut.delta
documentation:
Rate of change in option price
with respect to the forward price (1st derivative).
Note that this is the spot delta.
For the forward delta, use `forward_delta`.

methods.name: blackscholes.put.BlackScholesPut.spot_delta
documentation:
Delta discounted for interest rates.
For the forward delta, use `delta`.

methods.name: blackscholes.put.BlackScholesPut.dual_delta
documentation:
1st derivative in option price
with respect to strike price.

methods.name: blackscholes.put.BlackScholesPut.theta
documentation:
Rate of change in option price
with respect to time (i.e. time decay).

methods.name: blackscholes.put.BlackScholesPut.rho
documentation:
Rate of change in option price
with respect to the risk-free rate.

methods.name: blackscholes.put.BlackScholesPut.epsilon
documentation:
Change in option price with respect to underlying dividend yield. 

Also known as psi.

methods.name: blackscholes.put.BlackScholesPut.charm
documentation: Rate of change of delta over time (also known as delta decay).

methods.name: blackscholes.put.BlackScholesPut.in_the_money
documentation: Naive Probability that put option will be in the money at maturity.

Class to calculate (European) call option prices
and Greeks with the Black-Scholes-Merton formula.

```
param S

Price of underlying asset

param K

Strike price

param T

Time till expiration in years (1/12 indicates 1 month)

param r

Risk-free interest rate (0.05 indicates 5%)

param sigma

Volatility (standard deviation) of stock (0.15 indicates 15%)

param q

Annual dividend yield (0.05 indicates 5% yield)
```

methods.name: blackscholes.put.BinaryPut.rho
documentation:
Rate of change in option price
with respect to the risk-free rate.

Calculate (European) put option prices for a binary option.
Also called a digital or exotic option.

```
param S

Price of underlying asset

param K

Strike price

param T

Time till expiration in years (1/12 indicates 1 month)

param r

Risk-free interest rate (0.05 indicates 5%)

param sigma

Volatility (standard deviation) of stock (0.15 indicates 15%)
```

Assumes dividend yield is 0%.

classes.name: blackscholes.butterfly.BlackScholesButterflyLong

methods.name: blackscholes.butterfly.BlackScholesButterflyLong._calc_attr
documentation:
Combines attributes from three call options into a long call butterfly.

All greeks and price are combined in the same way.

```
param attribute_name

String name of option attribute
```

pointing to a method that can be called on
BlackScholesCall and BlackScholesPut.

```
return

Combined value according to long call butterfly.
```

Create long butterfly option structure.
\- Long butterfly -> Call(K1) - 2 * Call(K2) + Call(K3)

```
param S

Price of underlying asset

param K1

Strike price for 1st option

param K2

Strike price for 2nd option

param K3

Strike price for 3rd option
```

It must hold that K1 < K2 < K3.

Additionally, it must hold that K2 - K1 = K3 - K2

```
param T

Time till expiration in years (1/12 indicates 1 month)

param r

Risk-free interest rate (0.05 indicates 5%)

param sigma

Volatility (standard deviation) of stock (0.15 indicates 15%)

param q

Annual dividend yield (0.05 indicates 5% yield)
```

classes.name: blackscholes.butterfly.BlackScholesButterflyShort

methods.name: blackscholes.butterfly.BlackScholesButterflyShort._calc_attr
documentation:
Combines attributes from three put options into a short put butterfly.

All greeks and price are combined in the same way.

```
param attribute_name

String name of option attribute
```

pointing to a method that can be called on
BlackScholesCall and BlackScholesPut.

```
return

Combined value according to short put butterfly.
```

Create short butterfly option structure.

- Short butterfly -> -Put(K1) + 2 * Put(K2) - Put(K3)

```
param S

Price of underlying asset

param K1

Strike price for 1st option

param K2

Strike price for 2nd option

param K3

Strike price for 3rd option
```

It must hold that K1 < K2 < K3.

Additionally, it must hold that K2 - K1 = K3 - K2

```
param T

Time till expiration in years (1/12 indicates 1 month)

param r

Risk-free interest rate (0.05 indicates 5%)

param sigma

Volatility (standard deviation) of stock (0.15 indicates 15%)

param q

Annual dividend yield (0.05 indicates 5% yield)
```

classes.name: blackscholes.iron_condor.BlackScholesIronCondorLong

methods.name: blackscholes.iron_condor.BlackScholesIronCondorLong._calc_attr
documentation:
Combines attributes from two put and two call options into a long iron condor.

All greeks and price are combined in the same way.

```
param attribute_name

String name of option attribute
```

pointing to a method that can be called on
BlackScholesCall and BlackScholesPut.

```
return

Combined value according to long iron condor.
```

Create long iron condor option structure.

- Long iron condor -> Put(K1) - Put(K2) - Call(K3) + Call(K4)

```
param S

Price of underlying asset

param K1

Strike price for 1st option

param K2

Strike price for 2nd option

param K3

Strike price for 3rd option

param K4

Strike price for 3rd option
```

It must hold that K1 < K2 < K3 < K4.

Additionally, it must hold that K4 - K3 = K2 - K1

```
param T

Time till expiration in years (1/12 indicates 1 month)

param r

Risk-free interest rate (0.05 indicates 5%)

param sigma

Volatility (standard deviation) of stock (0.15 indicates 15%)

param q

Annual dividend yield (0.05 indicates 5% yield)
```

classes.name: blackscholes.iron_condor.BlackScholesIronCondorShort

methods.name: blackscholes.iron_condor.BlackScholesIronCondorShort._calc_attr
documentation:
Combines attributes from two put and two call options into a short iron condor.

All greeks and price are combined in the same way.

```
param attribute_name

String name of option attribute
```

pointing to a method that can be called on
BlackScholesCall and BlackScholesPut.

```
return

Combined value according to short iron condor.
```

Create short iron condor option structure.

- Short iron condor -> -Put(K1) + Put(K2) + Call(K3) - Call(K4)

```
param S

Price of underlying asset

param K1

Strike price for 1st option

param K2

Strike price for 2nd option

param K3

Strike price for 3rd option

param K4

Strike price for 3rd option
```

It must hold that K1 < K2 < K3 < K4.

Additionally, it must hold that K4 - K3 = K2 - K1

```
param T

Time till expiration in years (1/12 indicates 1 month)

param r

Risk-free interest rate (0.05 indicates 5%)

param sigma

Volatility (standard deviation) of stock (0.15 indicates 15%)

param q

Annual dividend yield (0.05 indicates 5% yield)
```

classes.name: blackscholes.iron_butterfly.BlackScholesIronButterflyLong

methods.name: blackscholes.iron_butterfly.BlackScholesIronButterflyLong._calc_attr
documentation:
Combines attributes from two put and two call options into a long iron butterfly.

All greeks and price are combined in the same way.

```
param attribute_name

String name of option attribute
```

pointing to a method that can be called on
BlackScholesCall and BlackScholesPut.

```
return

Combined value according to long iron butterfly.
```

Create long iron butterfly option structure.

- Long iron butterfly -> - Put(K1) + Put(K2) + Call(K3) - Call(K4)

```
param S

Price of underlying asset

param K1

Strike price for 1st option

param K2

Strike price for 2nd and 3rd option

param K3

Strike price for 4th option
```

It must hold that K1 < K2 < K3.

Additionally, it must hold that K3 - K2 = K2 - K1 (equidistant strike prices)

```
param T

Time till expiration in years (1/12 indicates 1 month)

param r

Risk-free interest rate (0.05 indicates 5%)

param sigma

Volatility (standard deviation) of stock (0.15 indicates 15%)

param q

Annual dividend yield (0.05 indicates 5% yield)
```

```

It must hold that K1 < K2 < K3.

Additionally, it must hold that K3 - K2 = K2 - K1 (equidistant strike prices)

```
param T

Time till expiration in years (1/12 indicates 1 month)

param r

Risk-free interest rate (0.05 indicates 5%)

param sigma

param q

Rate of change in option price

Percentage change in option value per %

documentation: Rate of change of delta over time (also known as delta decay).

methods.name: blackscholes.base.BlackScholesBase.veta
documentation: Rate of change in `vega` with respect to time.

methods.name: blackscholes.base.BlackScholesBase.phi
documentation:
2nd order partial derivative with respect to strike price. 

Phi is used in the Breeden-Litzenberger formula. 

Breeden-Litzenberger uses quoted option prices
to estimate risk-neutral probabilities.

documentation:
Sensitivity of vomma with respect to change in volatility. 

3rd order derivative of option value to volatility.

documentation:
Theta to gamma ratio. Also called "gamma rent".
More info: "Dynamic Hedging" by Nassim Taleb, p. 178-181.

Base functionality to calculate (European) prices
and Greeks with the Black-Scholes-Merton formula.

```
param S

Price of underlying asset

param K

Strike price

param T

Time till expiration in years (1/12 indicates 1 month)

param r

Risk-free interest rate (0.05 indicates 5%)

param sigma

Volatility (standard deviation) of stock (0.15 indicates 15%)

param q

Annual dividend yield (0.05 indicates 5% yield)
```

Base functionality to calculate (European) prices
and Greeks with the Black-76 formula.

This variant of the Black-Scholes-Merton model is
often used for pricing options on futures and bonds.

```
param F

Futures price

param K

Strike price

param T

Time till expiration in years (1/12 indicates 1 month)

param r

Risk-free interest rate (0.05 indicates 5%)

param sigma

Volatility (standard deviation) of stock (0.15 indicates 15%)
```

classes.name: blackscholes.base.BlackScholesStructureBase

methods.name: blackscholes.base.BlackScholesStructureBase._calc_attr
documentation:
Combines attributes from several put and call options.

Ex. Long Straddle

python
def \_calc_attr(self, attribute_name: str) -> float:

> put_attr = getattr(self.put1, attribute_name)
> call_attr = getattr(self.call1, attribute_name)
> return put_attr() + call_attr()

\`
In this way all greeks and price are combined in the same way.

In this case only simple addition is performed.

```
param attribute_name

String name of option attribute
```

pointing to a method that can be called on
BlackScholesCall and BlackScholesPut.

```
return

Combined value (float)
```

Base class for (European) binary options.
Also called a digital or exotic option.

```
param S

Price of underlying asset

param K

Strike price

param T

Time till expiration in years (1/12 indicates 1 month)

param r

Risk-free interest rate (0.05 indicates 5%)

param sigma

Volatility (standard deviation) of stock (0.15 indicates 15%)
```

Assumes dividend yield is 0%.
